normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem knapsack_spec (weights values : List Nat) (capacity : Nat)
  (hlen : weights.length = values.length)
  (hnonneg : True) : True := by trivial
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Set up the problem with equal-length lists of non-negative weights and values and a non-negative capacity.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
def feasibleSubsets (weights values : List Nat) (capacity : Nat) : Set (List Nat) :=
  { idxs |
    let n := weights.length
    idxs.Nodup ∧
    idxs ⊆ List.range n ∧
    (sum (idxs.map (fun i => weights.getD i 0))) ≤ capacity ∧
    (sum (idxs.map (fun i => values.getD i 0))) ≥ 0 }
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Define what it means for a subset (represented by distinct indices) to be feasible: indices are within bounds, no repeats, and total weight is within capacity.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
def subsetValue (weights values : List Nat) (idxs : List Nat) : Nat :=
  sum (idxs.map (fun i => values.getD i 0))
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Define the total value of a chosen subset (by indices).

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
def subsetWeight (weights values : List Nat) (idxs : List Nat) : Nat :=
  sum (idxs.map (fun i => weights.getD i 0))
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Define the total weight of a chosen subset (by indices).

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
def knapsackAnswer (weights values : List Nat) (capacity : Nat) : Nat :=
  sSup ( { subsetValue weights values idxs | idxs ∈ feasibleSubsets weights values capacity } )
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Define the intended output as the maximum total value among all feasible subsets.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem feasible_empty (weights values : List Nat) (capacity : Nat) :
  [] ∈ feasibleSubsets weights values capacity := by
  simp [feasibleSubsets, subsetWeight, subsetValue]
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: The empty subset is feasible (weight 0 ≤ capacity), so the maximum is well-defined.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem knapsackAnswer_is_upper_bound (weights values : List Nat) (capacity : Nat)
  (idxs : List Nat) (hfeas : idxs ∈ feasibleSubsets weights values capacity) :
  subsetValue weights values idxs ≤ knapsackAnswer weights values capacity := by
  -- follows from definition as supremum/maximum over feasible values
  admit
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Any feasible subset’s total value is ≤ the returned answer.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem knapsackAnswer_attains (weights values : List Nat) (capacity : Nat) :
  ∃ idxs : List Nat,
    idxs ∈ feasibleSubsets weights values capacity ∧
    subsetValue weights values idxs = knapsackAnswer weights values capacity := by
  -- for Nat, supremum over a finite set is attained
  admit
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: There exists a feasible subset whose value equals the returned maximum.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem feasible_implies_each_item_used_at_most_once (weights values : List Nat) (capacity : Nat)
  (idxs : List Nat) (hfeas : idxs ∈ feasibleSubsets weights values capacity) :
  idxs.Nodup := by
  simpa [feasibleSubsets] using (by
    have := (show idxs ∈ feasibleSubsets weights values capacity from hfeas)
    simpa [feasibleSubsets] using this)
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Feasibility enforces that each item index appears at most once (no repeated selection).

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem feasible_implies_weight_le_capacity (weights values : List Nat) (capacity : Nat)
  (idxs : List Nat) (hfeas : idxs ∈ feasibleSubsets weights values capacity) :
  subsetWeight weights values idxs ≤ capacity := by
  simpa [feasibleSubsets, subsetWeight] using (by
    have := (show idxs ∈ feasibleSubsets weights values capacity from hfeas)
    simpa [feasibleSubsets] using this)
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Any feasible subset respects the capacity constraint on total weight.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem feasible_implies_indices_within_length (weights values : List Nat) (capacity : Nat)
  (idxs : List Nat) (hfeas : idxs ∈ feasibleSubsets weights values capacity) :
  let n := weights.length
  idxs ⊆ List.range n := by
  simpa [feasibleSubsets] using (by
    have := (show idxs ∈ feasibleSubsets weights values capacity from hfeas)
    simpa [feasibleSubsets] using this)
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: Chosen indices correspond to valid items within the list length.

normalized_obligations.lean_statement:
theorem knapsack_correct_for_equal_lengths (weights values : List Nat) (capacity : Nat)
  (hlen : weights.length = values.length) :
  knapsackAnswer weights values capacity =
    sSup ( { subsetValue weights values idxs | idxs.Nodup ∧ idxs ⊆ List.range weights.length ∧ subsetWeight weights values idxs ≤ capacity } ) := by
  -- show feasibleSubsets matches the intended constraints when lengths agree
  admit
normalized_obligations.nl_gloss: When weights and values have equal length, the feasibility definition matches the intended subset constraints over items.
